a developer of a library written in C, one can utilize pkg-config during the configuration process to obtain the correct set of flags for a dependency. In my case, I help to maintain [FLINT](https://github.com/flintlib/flint/), and I want to check that our changes work for our users. This includes checking that [Oscar.jl](https://github.com/oscar-system/Oscar.jl)'s dependency [Nemo.jl](https://github.com/Nemocas/Nemo.jl/) works as intended with our changes, when built against Julia's version of GMP (located in `.../artifacts/...`).

However, when I ask for the right flags include flags for GMP (or for any binary that I have on my system, for that matter), I get
```bash
$ PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/home/albin/.julia/artifacts/4c207c7dc70b23755d9281a92daa9b8f11dc746d/lib/pkgconfig \
  pkg-config --cflags-only-I gmp
-I/workspace/destdir/include
```
which is wrong since everything should point to a directory under `artifacts`.

Could this be fixed? ```
$ julia

julia>                      # Opening Julia is quite slow, but no change here
julia> writing_some_stuff   # No delay with autocompletion or anything

(@v1.11) pkg>               # **Very** slow to open Pkg REPL-mode
(@v1.11) pkg> dev           # Starting to write "dev PACKAGE", delay after writing 'd'
(@v1.11) pkg> dev PACKAGE   # Initial delay after writing 'P', maybe 'A' as well

(@v1.11) pkg> help          # Very slow to complete this command

(@v1.11) pkg> dev PACKAGE   # The second time it is fast again
(@v1.11) pkg> help          # The second time it is fast again
```

Julia uses this function in `src/gc.c`, but never provides `jl_aligned_alloc` or something similar like it does with `jl_malloc`.

Now, my understanding is that Julia's memory allocator offsets the pointer obtained from `malloc` by 16 bytes, so a direct translation of `jl_malloc` to `jl_aligned_alloc` would not work. Moreover, Windows would require a separate function for freeing aligned memory.

Now, would it be reasonable to ask for something like `jl_aligned_alloc`?

For [FLINT](https://github.com/flintlib/flint), this would be useful as our current FFT code for big multiplications relies on page-aligned allocations. We will probably have to end up wrapping aligned allocations through the user-set allocation function, but I thought it would be a good idea to bring it up at least.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52901/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52901/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
